title: Refinery Hot Work Ignition Precursor Pattern
content: Near-miss at a refinery processing unit where hot work welding was authorized in Zone D while methane readings in adjacent Zone C showed a 2.8x baseline increase. The permit was approved without cross-referencing live gas monitoring data. Wind speed of 4.2 m/s was carrying vapor toward the hot work area. Investigation noted that the gas monitoring alarm and permit system operated independently with no automated correlation. Recommended action: implement compound risk detection that fuses gas monitoring with permit activity. This pattern preceded 2 documented gas ignition near-misses at comparable refinery facilities in the last 3 years.
